{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saba Shrine | image = [[File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = 6-2 Sōja-chō, Hōfu, Yamaguchi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|03|40.5|N|131|34|57.2|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | deity = | shrine_rank = [[Suō Province]] [[Sōja]]
Former [[Kensha]] (Prefectural Shrine) | established= | architecture= | alternate_names = Sōja Kanekiri-sha | festivals = [[April 2]] | rites = }} '''Saba Shrine''' (佐波神社, ''Saba Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Sōja-chō, [[Hōfu]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. It served as the [[Sōja]] (provincial shrine) of [[Suō Province]] and was classified as a [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == Situated at the southern foot of Tatara Mountain, the shrine venerates a pantheon of fourteen deities, including [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo]], [[Mihotsuhime|Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto]], [[Shinatsuhiko]], Shinatsuhime, [[Ukano Mitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsuo, Nakatsutsuo, Uwatsutsuo), [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Takeminakata]], [[Takemikazuchi]], and [[Futsunushi-no-Kami]]. Originally known as ''Sōja Kanekiri-sha,'' the shrine was not listed in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' and is classified as an [[Shikigai Shrine|unranked shrine]]. According to legend, Emperor [[Chūai]] and Empress [[Jingū]] enshrined the gods here to pray for victory during their campaign against the [[Kumaso]]. The name ''Kanekiri'' ("metal cutting") is thought to symbolize the act of pacifying (cutting down) resistance during the [[Three Han Campaign|expedition to the Three Han]] regions, reflecting the [[Yin-Yang Five Phases|Five Elements theory]] that associates Korea with the "metal" direction御園生翁甫編『防府市史上巻』pp. 196–197. As a [[Sōja]], the shrine was established as a central place for provincial worship where the deities of all local shrines could be venerated. The exact date of its establishment is unclear but is presumed to have been after the founding of the [[Suō Provincial Capital|provincial capital]]. In 1907, the shrine merged with the village shrines [[Katsuma Shrine]], [[Hiyoshi Shrine]], and [[Hachiman Shrine]], adopting the name ''Saba Shrine.'' In 1922, it was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine, and by 1924 it was designated to receive offerings from the state. Renovations to the shrine buildings were carried out during this period御園生翁甫編『続防府市史』pp. 244–246. The shrine grounds feature a memorial and song monument dedicated to the composer [[Nōshō Ōmura]], a Hōfu native. A commemorative festival is held annually on [[January 23]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}. == Grounds == Former [[Kensha]] (Prefectural Shrine) | established= | architecture= | alternate_names = Sōja Kanekiri-sha | festivals = [[April 2]] | rites = }} '''Saba Shrine''' (佐波神社, ''Saba Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Sōja-chō, [[Hōfu]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. It served as the [[Sōja]] (provincial shrine) of [[Suō Province]] and was classified as a [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == Situated at the southern foot of Tatara Mountain, the shrine venerates a pantheon of fourteen deities, including [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo]], [[Mihotsuhime|Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto]], [[Shinatsuhiko]], Shinatsuhime, [[Ukano Mitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsuo, Nakatsutsuo, Uwatsutsuo), [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Takeminakata]], [[Takemikazuchi]], and [[Futsunushi-no-Kami]]. Originally known as ''Sōja Kanekiri-sha,'' the shrine was not listed in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' and is classified as an [[Shikigai Shrine|unranked shrine]]. According to legend, Emperor [[Chūai]] and Empress [[Jingū]] enshrined the gods here to pray for victory during their campaign against the [[Kumaso]]. The name ''Kanekiri'' ("metal cutting") is thought to symbolize the act of pacifying (cutting down) resistance during the [[Three Han Campaign|expedition to the Three Han]] regions, reflecting the [[Yin-Yang Five Phases|Five Elements theory]] that associates Korea with the "metal" direction御園生翁甫編『防府市史上巻』pp. 196–197. As a [[Sōja]], the shrine was established as a central place for provincial worship where the deities of all local shrines could be venerated. The exact date of its establishment is unclear but is presumed to have been after the founding of the [[Suō Provincial Capital|provincial capital]]. In 1907, the shrine merged with the village shrines [[Katsuma Shrine]], [[Hiyoshi Shrine]], and [[Hachiman Shrine]], adopting the name ''Saba Shrine.'' In 1922, it was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine, and by 1924 it was designated to receive offerings from the state. Renovations to the shrine buildings were carried out during this period御園生翁甫編『続防府市史』pp. 244–246. The shrine grounds feature a memorial and song monument dedicated to the composer [[Nōshō Ōmura]], a Hōfu native. A commemorative festival is held annually on [[January 23]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}. == Grounds == File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden (main sanctuary) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), keidaisha.jpg|Keidaisha (sub-shrine for the Inari deity) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|Torii (shrine gate) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=防府市史上巻|publisher=防府市教育委員会|date=1956-03-20|pages=196–197}} * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=続防府市史|publisher=続防府市史刊行会|date=1960-11-06|pages=244–246}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Saba-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-substub|pref=山口県}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hōfu]] [[Category:Historical sites in Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Suō Province|Shrine 0]] [[Category:Saba Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:refreshing redirects 1-26]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Saba Shrine]] [[sn:aba Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:さばしんじゃ}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Saba Shrine}}

The shrine grounds feature a memorial and song monument dedicated to the composer [[Nōshō Ōmura]], a Hōfu native. Former [[Kensha]] (Prefectural Shrine) | established= | architecture= | alternate_names = Sōja Kanekiri-sha | festivals = [[April 2]] | rites = }} '''Saba Shrine''' (佐波神社, ''Saba Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Sōja-chō, [[Hōfu]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. It served as the [[Sōja]] (provincial shrine) of [[Suō Province]] and was classified as a [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == Situated at the southern foot of Tatara Mountain, the shrine venerates a pantheon of fourteen deities, including [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo]], [[Mihotsuhime|Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto]], [[Shinatsuhiko]], Shinatsuhime, [[Ukano Mitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsuo, Nakatsutsuo, Uwatsutsuo), [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Takeminakata]], [[Takemikazuchi]], and [[Futsunushi-no-Kami]]. Originally known as ''Sōja Kanekiri-sha,'' the shrine was not listed in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' and is classified as an [[Shikigai Shrine|unranked shrine]]. According to legend, Emperor [[Chūai]] and Empress [[Jingū]] enshrined the gods here to pray for victory during their campaign against the [[Kumaso]]. The name ''Kanekiri'' ("metal cutting") is thought to symbolize the act of pacifying (cutting down) resistance during the [[Three Han Campaign|expedition to the Three Han]] regions, reflecting the [[Yin-Yang Five Phases|Five Elements theory]] that associates Korea with the "metal" direction御園生翁甫編『防府市史上巻』pp. 196–197. As a [[Sōja]], the shrine was established as a central place for provincial worship where the deities of all local shrines could be venerated. The exact date of its establishment is unclear but is presumed to have been after the founding of the [[Suō Provincial Capital|provincial capital]]. In 1907, the shrine merged with the village shrines [[Katsuma Shrine]], [[Hiyoshi Shrine]], and [[Hachiman Shrine]], adopting the name ''Saba Shrine.'' In 1922, it was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine, and by 1924 it was designated to receive offerings from the state. Renovations to the shrine buildings were carried out during this period御園生翁甫編『続防府市史』pp. 244–246. The shrine grounds feature a memorial and song monument dedicated to the composer [[Nōshō Ōmura]], a Hōfu native. It served as the [[Sōja]] (provincial shrine) of [[Suō Province]] and was classified as a [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern system of ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == Situated at the southern foot of Tatara Mountain, the shrine venerates a pantheon of fourteen deities, including [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo]], [[Mihotsuhime|Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto]], [[Shinatsuhiko]], Shinatsuhime, [[Ukano Mitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsuo, Nakatsutsuo, Uwatsutsuo), [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Takeminakata]], [[Takemikazuchi]], and [[Futsunushi-no-Kami]]. Originally known as ''Sōja Kanekiri-sha,'' the shrine was not listed in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' and is classified as an [[Shikigai Shrine|unranked shrine]]. According to legend, Emperor [[Chūai]] and Empress [[Jingū]] enshrined the gods here to pray for victory during their campaign against the [[Kumaso]]. The name ''Kanekiri'' ("metal cutting") is thought to symbolize the act of pacifying (cutting down) resistance during the [[Three Han Campaign|expedition to the Three Han]] regions, reflecting the [[Yin-Yang Five Phases|Five Elements theory]] that associates Korea with the "metal" direction御園生翁甫編『防府市史上巻』pp. 196–197. As a [[Sōja]], the shrine was established as a central place for provincial worship where the deities of all local shrines could be venerated. The exact date of its establishment is unclear but is presumed to have been after the founding of the [[Suō Provincial Capital|provincial capital]]. In 1907, the shrine merged with the village shrines [[Katsuma Shrine]], [[Hiyoshi Shrine]], and [[Hachiman Shrine]], adopting the name ''Saba Shrine.'' In 1922, it was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine, and by 1924 it was designated to receive offerings from the state. Renovations to the shrine buildings were carried out during this period御園生翁甫編『続防府市史』pp. 244–246. The shrine grounds feature a memorial and song monument dedicated to the composer [[Nōshō Ōmura]], a Hōfu native. A commemorative festival is held annually on [[January 23]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}. == Grounds == File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden (main sanctuary) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), keidaisha.jpg|Keidaisha (sub-shrine for the Inari deity) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|Torii (shrine gate) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=防府市史上巻|publisher=防府市教育委員会|date=1956-03-20|pages=196–197}} * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=続防府市史|publisher=続防府市史刊行会|date=1960-11-06|pages=244–246}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Saba-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-substub|pref=山口県}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さばしんじゃ}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Saba Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hōfu]] [[Category:Historical sites in Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Suō Province|Shrine 0]] [[Category:Saba Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Saba Shrine]] [[sn:aba Shrine]]

Former [[Kensha]] (Prefectural Shrine) | established= | architecture= | alternate_names = Sōja Kanekiri-sha | festivals = [[April 2]] | rites = }} '''Saba Shrine''' (佐波神社, ''Saba Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Sōja-chō, [[Hōfu]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. It served as the [[Sōja]] (provincial shrine) of [[Suō Province]] and was classified as a [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == Situated at the southern foot of Tatara Mountain, the shrine venerates a pantheon of fourteen deities, including [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo]], [[Mihotsuhime|Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto]], [[Shinatsuhiko]], Shinatsuhime, [[Ukano Mitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsuo, Nakatsutsuo, Uwatsutsuo), [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi]], [[Takeminakata]], [[Takemikazuchi]], and [[Futsunushi-no-Kami]]. Originally known as ''Sōja Kanekiri-sha,'' the shrine was not listed in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' and is classified as an [[Shikigai Shrine|unranked shrine]]. According to legend, Emperor [[Chūai]] and Empress [[Jingū]] enshrined the gods here to pray for victory during their campaign against the [[Kumaso]]. The name ''Kanekiri'' ("metal cutting") is thought to symbolize the act of pacifying (cutting down) resistance during the [[Three Han Campaign|expedition to the Three Han]] regions, reflecting the [[Yin-Yang Five Phases|Five Elements theory]] that associates Korea with the "metal" direction御園生翁甫編『防府市史上巻』pp. 196–197. As a [[Sōja]], the shrine was established as a central place for provincial worship where the deities of all local shrines could be venerated. The exact date of its establishment is unclear but is presumed to have been after the founding of the [[Suō Provincial Capital|provincial capital]]. In 1907, the shrine merged with the village shrines [[Katsuma Shrine]], [[Hiyoshi Shrine]], and [[Hachiman Shrine]], adopting the name ''Saba Shrine.'' In 1922, it was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine, and by 1924 it was designated to receive offerings from the state. Renovations to the shrine buildings were carried out during this period御園生翁甫編『続防府市史』pp. 244–246. The shrine grounds feature a memorial and song monument dedicated to the composer [[Nōshō Ōmura]], a Hōfu native. A commemorative festival is held annually on [[January 23]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}. == Grounds == File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden (main sanctuary) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), keidaisha.jpg|Keidaisha (sub-shrine for the Inari deity) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|Torii (shrine gate) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=防府市史上巻|publisher=防府市教育委員会|date=1956-03-20|pages=196–197}} * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=続防府市史|publisher=続防府市史刊行会|date=1960-11-06|pages=244–246}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Saba-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-substub|pref=山口県}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hōfu]] [[Category:Historical sites in Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Suō Province|Shrine 0]] [[Category:Saba Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Saba Shrine]] [[sn:aba Shrine]]

It served as the [[Sōja]] (provincial shrine) of [[Suō Province]] and was classified as a [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == Situated at the southern foot of Tatara Mountain, the shrine venerates a pantheon of fourteen deities, including [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo]], [[Mihotsuhime|Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto]], [[Shinatsuhiko]], Shinatsuhime, [[Ukano Mitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsuo, Nakatsutsuo, Uwatsutsuo), [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Takeminakata]], [[Takemikazuchi]], and [[Futsunushi-no-Kami]]. Originally known as ''Sōja Kanekiri-sha,'' the shrine was not listed in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' and is classified as an [[Shikigai Shrine|unranked shrine]]. According to legend, Emperor [[Chūai]] and Empress [[Jingū]] enshrined the gods here to pray for victory during their campaign against the [[Kumaso]]. It served as the [[Sōja]] (provincial shrine) of [[Suō Province]] and was classified as a [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == Situated at the southern foot of Tatara Mountain, the shrine venerates a pantheon of fourteen deities, including [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Mihotsuhime|Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto]], [[Shinatsuhiko]], Shinatsuhime, [[Ukano Mitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsuo, Nakatsutsuo, Uwatsutsuo), [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Takeminakata]], [[Takemikazuchi]], and [[Futsunushi-no-Kami]]. Originally known as ''Sōja Kanekiri-sha,'' the shrine was not listed in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' and is classified as an [[Shikigai Shrine|unranked shrine]]. According to legend, Emperor [[Chūai]] and Empress [[Jingū]] enshrined the gods here to pray for victory during their campaign against the [[Kumaso]]. The name ''Kanekiri'' ("metal cutting") is thought to symbolize the act of pacifying (cutting down) resistance during the [[Three Han Campaign|expedition to the Three Han]] regions, reflecting the [[Yin-Yang Five Phases|Five Elements theory]] that associates Korea with the "metal" direction御園生翁甫編『防府市史上巻』pp. 196–197. As a [[Sōja]], the shrine was established as a central place for provincial worship where the deities of all local shrines could be venerated. The exact date of its establishment is unclear but is presumed to have been after the founding of the [[Suō Provincial Capital|provincial capital]]. In 1907, the shrine merged with the village shrines [[Katsuma Shrine]], [[Hiyoshi Shrine]], and [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], adopting the name ''Saba Shrine.'' In 1922, it was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine, and by 1924 it was designated to receive offerings from the state. Renovations to the shrine buildings were carried out during this period御園生翁甫編『続防府市史』pp. 244–246. The shrine grounds feature a memorial and song monument dedicated to the composer [[Nōshō Ōmura]], a Hōfu native. A commemorative festival is held annually on [[January 23]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}. == Grounds == File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden (main sanctuary) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), keidaisha.jpg|Keidaisha (sub-shrine for the Inari deity) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|Torii (shrine gate) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=防府市史上巻|publisher=防府市教育委員会|date=1956-03-20|pages=196–197}} * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=続防府市史|publisher=続防府市史刊行会|date=1960-11-06|pages=244–246}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Saba-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-substub|pref=山口県}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Suō Province|Shrine 0]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Saba Shrine]] [[sn:aba Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saba Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]]

revision.comment: 5 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:佐波神社 (防府市) up to rev 97878770
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 佐波神社 |画像 = [[File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 |所在地 = 山口県防府市惣社町6番2号 | 緯度度 = 34|緯度分 = 3|緯度秒 = 40.5 | 経度度 =131|経度分 =34|経度秒 = 57.2 |地図 = Japan Yamaguchi |祭神 = |神体 = |社格 = [[周防国]][[総社]]
旧[[県社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 惣社金切社 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月2日]] |神事 = }} '''佐波神社'''（さばじんじゃ）は、[[山口県]][[防府市]]惣社町にある[[神社]]。[[周防国]][[総社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 歴史 == 多々良山の南麓に鎮座し、[[天照大神]]・[[素盞嗚尊]]・[[三穂津姫|三穂津姫命]]・[[シナツヒコ|級長津彦命]]・級長津姫命・[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]・[[住吉三神|底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命]]・[[大国主|大己貴命]]・[[事代主]]・[[タケミナカタ|建御名方神]]・[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌神]]・[[経津主神]]の十四神を祀る。 かつては惣社金切社といい、[[延喜式神名帳]]への記載がない式外神社であった。[[仲哀天皇]]と[[神功皇后]]が[[熊襲]]親征の際に戦勝祈願のために諸神を祀り、惣社宮となった。その後の[[三韓征伐]]の際に、[[三韓]]は[[陰陽五行思想]]によると「金」の方角にあたり、金を切り平げたと言う意味で金切社と称するようになったと伝えられている『防府市史上巻』p196-197。 [[国司]]が赴任した際に、その国中の神社に参詣するしきたりであったが、容易なことではないため、[[国府]]にその国の諸神を祀る惣社（[[総社]]）を建立する制度ができた。このことから、建立時期は不詳であるが[[周防国衙跡|周防国府]]設立後と考えられている。 [[1907年]]（[[明治]]40年）6月3日、金切社に村社の勝間神社・日吉神社・八幡宮を合併し佐波神社と改称。[[1922年]]（[[大正]]11年）4月29日に県社に列し、[[1924年]]5月2日には神饌幣帛料供進社に指定された。この年に社殿を改築している『続防府市史』p244 境内には、防府市出身の作曲家[[大村能章]]の顕彰碑と歌碑があり、毎年[[1月23日]]には命日祭が行われる{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}。 == 境内 == File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|本殿 File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), keidaisha.jpg|境内の鐘栄稲荷神社 File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|鳥居 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|author = 御園生翁甫|date = 1956-3-20|title =防府市史上巻|publisher= 防府市教育委員会|pages = 196-197}} * {{Cite book|和書|author = 御園生翁甫|date = 1960-11-6|title = 続防府市史|publisher= 続防府市史刊行会|pages = 244-246}} == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Saba-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-substub|pref=山口県}} {{デフォルトソート:さはしんしや}} [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:防府市の歴史]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]] [[Category:総社]] [[Category:周防国|社0]]

旧[[県社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = 惣社金切社 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[4月2日]] |神事 = }} '''佐波神社'''（さばじんじゃ）は、[[山口県]][[防府市]]惣社町にある[[神社]]。[[周防国]][[総社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。 == 歴史 == 多々良山の南麓に鎮座し、[[天照大神]]・[[素盞嗚尊]]・[[三穂津姫|三穂津姫命]]・[[シナツヒコ|級長津彦命]]・級長津姫命・[[ウカノミタマ|倉稲魂命]]・[[住吉三神|底筒男命・中筒男命・表筒男命]]・[[大国主|大己貴命]]・[[事代主]]・[[タケミナカタ|建御名方神]]・[[タケミカヅチ|武甕槌神]]・[[経津主神]]の十四神を祀る。 かつては惣社金切社といい、[[延喜式神名帳]]への記載がない式外神社であった。[[仲哀天皇]]と[[神功皇后]]が[[熊襲]]親征の際に戦勝祈願のために諸神を祀り、惣社宮となった。その後の[[三韓征伐]]の際に、[[三韓]]は[[陰陽五行思想]]によると「金」の方角にあたり、金を切り平げたと言う意味で金切社と称するようになったと伝えられている『防府市史上巻』p196-197。 [[国司]]が赴任した際に、その国中の神社に参詣するしきたりであったが、容易なことではないため、[[国府]]にその国の諸神を祀る惣社（[[総社]]）を建立する制度ができた。このことから、建立時期は不詳であるが[[周防国衙跡|周防国府]]設立後と考えられている。 [[1907年]]（[[明治]]40年）6月3日、金切社に村社の勝間神社・日吉神社・八幡宮を合併し佐波神社と改称。[[1922年]]（[[大正]]11年）4月29日に県社に列し、[[1924年]]5月2日には神饌幣帛料供進社に指定された。この年に社殿を改築している『続防府市史』p244 境内には、防府市出身の作曲家[[大村能章]]の顕彰碑と歌碑があり、毎年[[1月23日]]には命日祭が行われる{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}。 == 境内 == File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|本殿 File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), keidaisha.jpg|境内の鐘栄稲荷神社 File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|鳥居 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|author = 御園生翁甫|date = 1956-3-20|title =防府市史上巻|publisher= 防府市教育委員会|pages = 196-197}} * {{Cite book|和書|author = 御園生翁甫|date = 1960-11-6|title = 続防府市史|publisher= 続防府市史刊行会|pages = 244-246}} == 外部リンク == * {{Commonscat-inline|Saba-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-substub|pref=山口県}} {{デフォルトソート:さはしんしや}} [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:防府市の歴史]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]] [[Category:総社]] [[Category:周防国|社0]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saba Shrine | image = [[File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = 6-2 Sōja-chō, Hōfu, Yamaguchi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|03|40.5|N|131|34|57.2|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | deity = | shrine_Rank = [[Suō Province]] [[Sōja]]
Former [[Kensha]] (Prefectural Shrine) | established= | architecture= | alternate_names = Sōja Kanekiri-sha | festivals = [[:en:April 2|April 2]] | rites = }} '''Saba Shrine''' (佐波神社, ''Saba Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Sōja-chō, [[Hōfu]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. It served as the [[Sōja]] (provincial shrine) of [[Suō Province]] and was classified as a [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == Situated at the southern foot of Tatara Mountain, the shrine venerates a pantheon of fourteen deities, including [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Mihotsuhime|Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto]], [[Shinatsuhiko]], Shinatsuhime, [[Ukano Mitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsuo, Nakatsutsuo, Uwatsutsuo), [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Takeminakata]], [[Takemikazuchi]], and [[Futsunushi-no-Kami]]. Originally known as ''Sōja Kanekiri-sha,'' the shrine was not listed in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' and is classified as an [[Shikigai Shrine|unranked shrine]]. According to legend, Emperor [[Chūai]] and Empress [[Jingū]] enshrined the gods here to pray for victory during their campaign against the [[Kumaso]]. The name ''Kanekiri'' ("metal cutting") is thought to symbolize the act of pacifying (cutting down) resistance during the [[Three Han Campaign|expedition to the Three Han]] regions, reflecting the [[Yin-Yang Five Phases|Five Elements theory]] that associates Korea with the "metal" direction御園生翁甫編『防府市史上巻』pp. 196–197. As a [[Sōja]], the shrine was established as a central place for provincial worship where the deities of all local shrines could be venerated. The exact date of its establishment is unclear but is presumed to have been after the founding of the [[Suō Provincial Capital|provincial capital]]. In 1907, the shrine merged with the village shrines [[Katsuma Shrine]], [[Hiyoshi Shrine]], and [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], adopting the name ''Saba Shrine.'' In 1922, it was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine, and by 1924 it was designated to receive offerings from the state. Renovations to the shrine buildings were carried out during this period御園生翁甫編『続防府市史』pp. 244–246. The shrine grounds feature a memorial and song monument dedicated to the composer [[Nōshō Ōmura]], a Hōfu native. A commemorative festival is held annually on [[January 23]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}. == Grounds == File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden (main sanctuary) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), keidaisha.jpg|Keidaisha (sub-shrine for the Inari deity) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|Torii (shrine gate) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=防府市史上巻|publisher=防府市教育委員会|date=1956-03-20|pages=196–197}} * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=続防府市史|publisher=続防府市史刊行会|date=1960-11-06|pages=244–246}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Saba-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-substub|pref=山口県}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Suō Province|Shrine 0]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Saba Shrine]] [[sn:aba Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saba Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] {{translated page|ja|佐波神社 (防府市)|version=97878770|comment=Imported full ja history}}

The shrine grounds feature a memorial and song monument dedicated to the composer [[Nōshō Ōmura]], a Hōfu native. A commemorative festival is held annually on [[January 23]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}. == Grounds == File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden (main sanctuary) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), keidaisha.jpg|Keidaisha (sub-shrine for the Inari deity) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|Torii (shrine gate) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=防府市史上巻|publisher=防府市教育委員会|date=1956-03-20|pages=196–197}} * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=続防府市史|publisher=続防府市史刊行会|date=1960-11-06|pages=244–246}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Saba-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-substub|pref=山口県}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Suō Province|Shrine 0]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Saba Shrine]] [[sn:aba Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saba Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] {{translated page|ja|佐波神社 (防府市)|version=97878770|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saba Shrine | image = [[File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = 6-2 Sōja-chō, Hōfu, Yamaguchi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|03|40.5|N|131|34|57.2|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | deity = | shrine_Rank = [[Suō Province]] [[Sōja]]
Former [[Kensha]] (Prefectural Shrine) | established= | architecture= | alternate_names = Sōja Kanekiri-sha | festivals = [[:en:April 2|April 2]] | rites = }} '''Saba Shrine''' (佐波神社, ''Saba Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Sōja-chō, [[Hōfu]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. It served as the [[Sōja]] (provincial shrine) of [[Suō Province]] and was classified as a [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == Situated at the southern foot of Tatara Mountain, the shrine venerates a pantheon of fourteen deities, including [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Mihotsuhime|Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto]], [[Shinatsuhiko]], Shinatsuhime, [[Ukano Mitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsuo, Nakatsutsuo, Uwatsutsuo), [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Takeminakata]], [[Takemikazuchi]], and [[Futsunushi-no-Kami]]. Originally known as ''Sōja Kanekiri-sha,'' the shrine was not listed in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' and is classified as an [[Shikigai Shrine|unranked shrine]]. According to legend, Emperor [[Chūai]] and Empress [[Jingū]] enshrined the gods here to pray for victory during their campaign against the [[Kumaso]]. The name ''Kanekiri'' ("metal cutting") is thought to symbolize the act of pacifying (cutting down) resistance during the [[Three Han Campaign|expedition to the Three Han]] regions, reflecting the [[Yin-Yang Five Phases|Five Elements theory]] that associates Korea with the "metal" direction御園生翁甫編『防府市史上巻』pp. 196–197. As a [[Sōja]], the shrine was established as a central place for provincial worship where the deities of all local shrines could be venerated. The exact date of its establishment is unclear but is presumed to have been after the founding of the [[Suō Provincial Capital|provincial capital]]. In 1907, the shrine merged with the village shrines [[Katsuma Shrine]], [[Hiyoshi Shrine]], and [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], adopting the name ''Saba Shrine.'' In 1922, it was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine, and by 1924 it was designated to receive offerings from the state. Renovations to the shrine buildings were carried out during this period御園生翁甫編『続防府市史』pp. 244–246. The shrine grounds feature a memorial and song monument dedicated to the composer [[Nōshō Ōmura]], a Hōfu native. A commemorative festival is held annually on [[January 23]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}. == Grounds == File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden (main sanctuary) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), keidaisha.jpg|Keidaisha (sub-shrine for the Inari deity) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|Torii (shrine gate) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=防府市史上巻|publisher=防府市教育委員会|date=1956-03-20|pages=196–197}} * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=続防府市史|publisher=続防府市史刊行会|date=1960-11-06|pages=244–246}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Saba-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-substub|pref=山口県}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Suō Province|Shrine 0]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Saba Shrine]] [[sn:aba Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saba Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] {{translated page|ja|佐波神社 (防府市)|version=97878770|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]]

A commemorative festival is held annually on [[January 23]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}. == Grounds == File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden (main sanctuary) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), keidaisha.jpg|Keidaisha (sub-shrine for the Inari deity) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|Torii (shrine gate) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=防府市史上巻|publisher=防府市教育委員会|date=1956-03-20|pages=196–197}} * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=続防府市史|publisher=続防府市史刊行会|date=1960-11-06|pages=244–246}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Saba-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-substub|pref=山口県}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Suō Province|Shrine 0]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Saba Shrine]] [[sn:aba Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saba Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|佐波神社 (防府市)|version=97878770|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:防府市の歴史]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saba Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saba Shrine | image = [[File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = 6-2 Sōja-chō, Hōfu, Yamaguchi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|03|40.5|N|131|34|57.2|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | deity = | shrine_Rank = [[Suō Province]] [[Sōja]]
Former [[Kensha]] (Prefectural Shrine) | established= | architecture= | alternate_names = Sōja Kanekiri-sha | festivals = [[:en:April 2|April 2]] | rites = }} '''Saba Shrine''' (佐波神社, ''Saba Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Sōja-chō, [[Hōfu]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. It served as the [[Sōja]] (provincial shrine) of [[Suō Province]] and was classified as a [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == Situated at the southern foot of Tatara Mountain, the shrine venerates a pantheon of fourteen deities, including [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Mihotsuhime|Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto]], [[Shinatsuhiko]], Shinatsuhime, [[Ukano Mitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsuo, Nakatsutsuo, Uwatsutsuo), [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Takeminakata]], [[Takemikazuchi]], and [[Futsunushi-no-Kami]]. Originally known as ''Sōja Kanekiri-sha,'' the shrine was not listed in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' and is classified as an [[Shikigai Shrine|unranked shrine]]. According to legend, Emperor [[Chūai]] and Empress [[Jingū]] enshrined the gods here to pray for victory during their campaign against the [[Kumaso]]. The name ''Kanekiri'' ("metal cutting") is thought to symbolize the act of pacifying (cutting down) resistance during the [[Three Han Campaign|expedition to the Three Han]] regions, reflecting the [[Yin-Yang Five Phases|Five Elements theory]] that associates Korea with the "metal" direction御園生翁甫編『防府市史上巻』pp. 196–197. As a [[Sōja]], the shrine was established as a central place for provincial worship where the deities of all local shrines could be venerated. The exact date of its establishment is unclear but is presumed to have been after the founding of the [[Suō Provincial Capital|provincial capital]]. In 1907, the shrine merged with the village shrines [[Katsuma Shrine]], [[:en:Hiyoshi Shrine|Hiyoshi Shrine]], and [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], adopting the name ''Saba Shrine.'' In 1922, it was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine, and by 1924 it was designated to receive offerings from the state. Renovations to the shrine buildings were carried out during this period御園生翁甫編『続防府市史』pp. 244–246. The shrine grounds feature a memorial and song monument dedicated to the composer [[Nōshō Ōmura]], a Hōfu native. A commemorative festival is held annually on [[January 23]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}. == Grounds == File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden (main sanctuary) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), keidaisha.jpg|Keidaisha (sub-shrine for the Inari deity) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|Torii (shrine gate) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=防府市史上巻|publisher=防府市教育委員会|date=1956-03-20|pages=196–197}} * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=続防府市史|publisher=続防府市史刊行会|date=1960-11-06|pages=244–246}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Saba-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-substub|pref=山口県}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Suō Province|Shrine 0]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Saba Shrine]] [[sn:aba Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saba Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|佐波神社 (防府市)|version=97878770|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:防府市の歴史]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saba Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

A commemorative festival is held annually on [[:en:January 23|January 23]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}. == Grounds == File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden (main sanctuary) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), keidaisha.jpg|Keidaisha (sub-shrine for the Inari deity) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|Torii (shrine gate) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=防府市史上巻|publisher=防府市教育委員会|date=1956-03-20|pages=196–197}} * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=続防府市史|publisher=続防府市史刊行会|date=1960-11-06|pages=244–246}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Saba-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-substub|pref=山口県}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Suō Province|Shrine 0]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Saba Shrine]] [[sn:aba Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saba Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|佐波神社 (防府市)|version=97878770|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:防府の Municipal History]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saba Shrine]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saba Shrine | image = [[File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = 6-2 Sōja-chō, Hōfu, Yamaguchi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|03|40.5|N|131|34|57.2|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | deity = | shrine_Rank = [[Suō Province]] [[Sōja]]
Former [[Kensha]] (Prefectural Shrine) | established= | architecture= | alternate_names = Sōja Kanekiri-sha | festivals = [[:en:April 2|April 2]] | rites = }} '''Saba Shrine''' (佐波神社, ''Saba Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Sōja-chō, [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu]], [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. It served as the [[Sōja]] (provincial shrine) of [[Suō Province]] and was classified as a [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == Situated at the southern foot of Tatara Mountain, the shrine venerates a pantheon of fourteen deities, including [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Mihotsuhime|Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto]], [[Shinatsuhiko]], Shinatsuhime, [[Ukano Mitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsuo, Nakatsutsuo, Uwatsutsuo), [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Takeminakata]], [[Takemikazuchi]], and [[Futsunushi-no-Kami]]. Originally known as ''Sōja Kanekiri-sha,'' the shrine was not listed in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' and is classified as an [[Shikigai Shrine|unranked shrine]]. According to legend, Emperor [[Chūai]] and Empress [[:en:Jingū|Jingū]] enshrined the gods here to pray for victory during their campaign against the [[Kumaso]]. The name ''Kanekiri'' ("metal cutting") is thought to symbolize the act of pacifying (cutting down) resistance during the [[Three Han Campaign|expedition to the Three Han]] regions, reflecting the [[Yin-Yang Five Phases|Five Elements theory]] that associates Korea with the "metal" direction御園生翁甫編『防府市史上巻』pp. 196–197. As a [[Sōja]], the shrine was established as a central place for provincial worship where the deities of all local shrines could be venerated. The exact date of its establishment is unclear but is presumed to have been after the founding of the [[Suō Provincial Capital|provincial capital]]. In 1907, the shrine merged with the village shrines [[Katsuma Shrine]], [[:en:Hiyoshi Shrine|Hiyoshi Shrine]], and [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], adopting the name ''Saba Shrine.'' In 1922, it was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine, and by 1924 it was designated to receive offerings from the state. Renovations to the shrine buildings were carried out during this period御園生翁甫編『続防府市史』pp. 244–246. The shrine grounds feature a memorial and song monument dedicated to the composer [[Nōshō Ōmura]], a Hōfu native. A commemorative festival is held annually on [[:en:January 23|January 23]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}. == Grounds == File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden (main sanctuary) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), keidaisha.jpg|Keidaisha (sub-shrine for the Inari deity) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|Torii (shrine gate) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=防府市史上巻|publisher=防府市教育委員会|date=1956-03-20|pages=196–197}} * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=続防府市史|publisher=続防府市史刊行会|date=1960-11-06|pages=244–246}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Saba-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-substub|pref=山口県}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Suō Province|Shrine 0]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Saba Shrine]] [[sn:aba Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[sn:Saba Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without valid translation templates or en interwikis or ja interwikis]] [[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|佐波神社 (防府市)|version=97878770|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]] [[Category:防府市の建築物]] [[Category:防府の Municipal History]] [[sn:Saba Shrine]]

A commemorative festival is held annually on [[:en:January 23|January 23]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}. == Grounds == File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden (main sanctuary) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), keidaisha.jpg|Keidaisha (sub-shrine for the Inari deity) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|Torii (shrine gate) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=防府市史上巻|publisher=防府市教育委員会|date=1956-03-20|pages=196–197}} * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=続防府市史|publisher=続防府市史刊行会|date=1960-11-06|pages=244–246}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Saba-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-substub|pref=山口県}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Suō Province|Shrine 0]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Saba Shrine]] [[sn:aba Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[sn:Saba Shrine]] [[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|佐波神社 (防府市)|version=97878770|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Hōfu City]] [[Category:防府の Municipal History]] [[sn:Saba Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saba Shrine | image = [[File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = 6-2 Sōja-chō, Hōfu, Yamaguchi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|03|40.5|N|131|34|57.2|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | deity = | shrine_Rank = [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]] [[Sōja]]
Former [[Kensha]] (Prefectural Shrine) | established= | architecture= | alternate_names = Sōja Kanekiri-sha | festivals = [[:en:April 2|April 2]] | rites = }} '''Saba Shrine''' (佐波神社, ''Saba Jinja'') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Sōja-chō, [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. It served as the [[Sōja]] (provincial shrine) of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]] and was classified as a [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == Situated at the southern foot of Tatara Mountain, the shrine venerates a pantheon of fourteen deities, including [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Mihotsuhime|Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto]], [[Shinatsuhiko]], Shinatsuhime, [[Ukano Mitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsuo, Nakatsutsuo, Uwatsutsuo), [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Takeminakata]], [[Takemikazuchi]], and [[Futsunushi-no-Kami]]. Originally known as ''Sōja Kanekiri-sha,'' the shrine was not listed in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' and is classified as an [[Shikigai Shrine|unranked shrine]]. According to legend, Emperor [[Chūai]] and Empress [[:en:Jingū|Jingū]] enshrined the gods here to pray for victory during their campaign against the [[Kumaso]]. The name ''Kanekiri'' ("metal cutting") is thought to symbolize the act of pacifying (cutting down) resistance during the [[Three Han Campaign|expedition to the Three Han]] regions, reflecting the [[Yin-Yang Five Phases|Five Elements theory]] that associates Korea with the "metal" direction御園生翁甫編『防府市史上巻』pp. 196–197. As a [[Sōja]], the shrine was established as a central place for provincial worship where the deities of all local shrines could be venerated. The exact date of its establishment is unclear but is presumed to have been after the founding of the [[Suō Provincial Capital|provincial capital]]. In 1907, the shrine merged with the village shrines [[Katsuma Shrine]], [[:en:Hiyoshi Shrine|Hiyoshi Shrine]], and [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], adopting the name ''Saba Shrine.'' In 1922, it was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine, and by 1924 it was designated to receive offerings from the state. Renovations to the shrine buildings were carried out during this period御園生翁甫編『続防府市史』pp. 244–246. The shrine grounds feature a memorial and song monument dedicated to the composer [[Nōshō Ōmura]], a Hōfu native. A commemorative festival is held annually on [[:en:January 23|January 23]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}. == Grounds == File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden (main sanctuary) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), keidaisha.jpg|Keidaisha (sub-shrine for the Inari deity) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|Torii (shrine gate) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=防府市史上巻|publisher=防府市教育委員会|date=1956-03-20|pages=196–197}} * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=続防府市史|publisher=続防府市史刊行会|date=1960-11-06|pages=244–246}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Saba-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-substub|pref=山口県}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Suō Province|Shrine 0]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Saba Shrine]] [[sn:aba Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[sn:Saba Shrine]] [[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|佐波神社 (防府市)|version=97878770|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Hōfu City]] [[Category:防府の Municipal History]] [[sn:Saba Shrine]]

A commemorative festival is held annually on [[:en:January 23|January 23]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}. == Grounds == File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden (main sanctuary) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), keidaisha.jpg|Keidaisha (sub-shrine for the Inari deity) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|Torii (shrine gate) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=防府市史上巻|publisher=防府市教育委員会|date=1956-03-20|pages=196–197}} * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=続防府市史|publisher=続防府市史刊行会|date=1960-11-06|pages=244–246}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Saba-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-substub|pref=山口県}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Suō Province|Shrine 0]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Saba Shrine]] [[sn:aba Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[sn:Saba Shrine]] [[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|佐波神社 (防府市)|version=97878770|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Hōfu City]] [[Category:防府の Municipal History]] [[sn:Saba Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saba Shrine | image = [[File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg|280px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | location = 6-2 Sōja-chō, Hōfu, Yamaguchi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|03|40.5|N|131|34|57.2|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | deity = | shrine_Rank = [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]] [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]]
Former [[Kensha]] (Prefectural Shrine) | established= | architecture= | alternate_names = Sōja Kanekiri-sha | festivals = [[:en:April 2|April 2]] | rites = }} '''Saba Shrine''' (佐波神社, ''Saba Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Sōja-chō, [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. It served as the [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] (provincial shrine) of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]] and was classified as a [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == Situated at the southern foot of Tatara Mountain, the shrine venerates a pantheon of fourteen deities, including [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Mihotsuhime|Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Shinatsuhiko|Shinatsuhiko]], Shinatsuhime, [[Ukano Mitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsuo, Nakatsutsuo, Uwatsutsuo), [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Takeminakata]], [[Takemikazuchi]], and [[Futsunushi-no-Kami]]. Originally known as ''Sōja Kanekiri-sha,'' the shrine was not listed in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' and is classified as an [[Shikigai Shrine|unranked shrine]]. According to legend, Emperor [[Chūai]] and Empress [[:en:Jingū|Jingū]] enshrined the gods here to pray for victory during their campaign against the [[Kumaso]]. The name ''Kanekiri'' ("metal cutting") is thought to symbolize the act of pacifying (cutting down) resistance during the [[Three Han Campaign|expedition to the Three Han]] regions, reflecting the [[Yin-Yang Five Phases|Five Elements theory]] that associates Korea with the "metal" direction御園生翁甫編『防府市史上巻』pp. 196–197. As a [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]], the shrine was established as a central place for provincial worship where the deities of all local shrines could be venerated. The exact date of its establishment is unclear but is presumed to have been after the founding of the [[:en:Suō Provincial Capital|provincial capital]]. In 1907, the shrine merged with the village shrines [[Katsuma Shrine]], [[:en:Hiyoshi Shrine|Hiyoshi Shrine]], and [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], adopting the name ''Saba Shrine.'' In 1922, it was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine, and by 1924 it was designated to receive offerings from the state. Renovations to the shrine buildings were carried out during this period御園生翁甫編『続防府市史』pp. 244–246. The shrine grounds feature a memorial and song monument dedicated to the composer [[Nōshō Ōmura]], a Hōfu native. A commemorative festival is held annually on [[:en:January 23|January 23]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}. == Grounds == File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden (main sanctuary) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), keidaisha.jpg|Keidaisha (sub-shrine for the Inari deity) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|Torii (shrine gate) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=防府市史上巻|publisher=防府市教育委員会|date=1956-03-20|pages=196–197}} * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=続防府市史|publisher=続防府市史刊行会|date=1960-11-06|pages=244–246}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Saba-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-substub|pref=山口県}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Suō Province|Shrine 0]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Saba Shrine]] [[sn:aba Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[sn:Saba Shrine]] [[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|佐波神社 (防府市)|version=97878770|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Hōfu City]] [[Category:History of Hofu City]] [[sn:Saba Shrine]]

It served as the [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] (provincial shrine) of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]] and was classified as a [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == Situated at the southern foot of Tatara Mountain, the shrine venerates a pantheon of fourteen deities, including [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Mihotsuhime|Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Shinatsuhiko|Shinatsuhiko]], Shinatsuhime, [[Ukano Mitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsuo, Nakatsutsuo, Uwatsutsuo), [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Takeminakata]], [[Takemikazuchi]], and [[Futsunushi-no-Kami]]. Originally known as ''Sōja Kanekiri-sha,'' the shrine was not listed in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' and is classified as an [[Shikigai Shrine|unranked shrine]]. According to legend, Emperor [[Chūai]] and Empress [[:en:Jingū|Jingū]] enshrined the gods here to pray for victory during their campaign against the [[Kumaso]]. As a [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]], the shrine was established as a central place for provincial worship where the deities of all local shrines could be venerated. The exact date of its establishment is unclear but is presumed to have been after the founding of the [[:en:Suō Provincial Capital|provincial capital]]. In 1907, the shrine merged with the village shrines [[Katsuma Shrine]], [[:en:Hiyoshi Shrine|Hiyoshi Shrine]], and [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], adopting the name ''Saba Shrine.'' In 1922, it was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine, and by 1924 it was designated to receive offerings from the state. Renovations to the shrine buildings were carried out during this period御園生翁甫編『続防府市史』pp. 244–246. The shrine grounds feature a memorial and song monument dedicated to the composer [[Nōshō Ōmura]], a Hōfu native. A commemorative festival is held annually on [[:en:January 23|January 23]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}. == Grounds == File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden (main sanctuary) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), keidaisha.jpg|Keidaisha (sub-shrine for the Inari deity) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|Torii (shrine gate) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=防府市史上巻|publisher=防府市教育委員会|date=1956-03-20|pages=196–197}} * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=続防府市史|publisher=続防府市史刊行会|date=1960-11-06|pages=244–246}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Saba-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-substub|pref=山口県}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Suō Province|Shrine 0]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|佐波神社 (防府市)|version=97878770|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Hōfu City]] [[Category:History of Hofu City]] {{wikidata link|Q38276448}}

Former [[Kensha]] (Prefectural Shrine) | established= | architecture= | alternate_names = Sōja Kanekiri-sha | festivals = [[:en:April 2|April 2]] | rites = | prefecture= {{ill|Hofu|WD=Q502209}} }} '''Saba Shrine''' (佐波神社, ''Saba Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Sōja-chō, [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. It served as the [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] (provincial shrine) of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]] and was classified as a [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == Situated at the southern foot of Tatara Mountain, the shrine venerates a pantheon of fourteen deities, including [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Mihotsuhime|Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Shinatsuhiko|Shinatsuhiko]], Shinatsuhime, [[Ukano Mitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsuo, Nakatsutsuo, Uwatsutsuo), [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Takeminakata]], [[Takemikazuchi]], and [[Futsunushi-no-Kami]]. Originally known as ''Sōja Kanekiri-sha,'' the shrine was not listed in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' and is classified as an [[Shikigai Shrine|unranked shrine]]. According to legend, Emperor [[Chūai]] and Empress [[:en:Jingū|Jingū]] enshrined the gods here to pray for victory during their campaign against the [[Kumaso]]. The name ''Kanekiri'' ("metal cutting") is thought to symbolize the act of pacifying (cutting down) resistance during the [[Three Han Campaign|expedition to the Three Han]] regions, reflecting the [[Yin-Yang Five Phases|Five Elements theory]] that associates Korea with the "metal" direction御園生翁甫編『防府市史上巻』pp. 196–197. As a [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]], the shrine was established as a central place for provincial worship where the deities of all local shrines could be venerated. The exact date of its establishment is unclear but is presumed to have been after the founding of the [[:en:Suō Provincial Capital|provincial capital]]. In 1907, the shrine merged with the village shrines [[Katsuma Shrine]], [[:en:Hiyoshi Shrine|Hiyoshi Shrine]], and [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], adopting the name ''Saba Shrine.'' In 1922, it was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine, and by 1924 it was designated to receive offerings from the state. Renovations to the shrine buildings were carried out during this period御園生翁甫編『続防府市史』pp. 244–246. The shrine grounds feature a memorial and song monument dedicated to the composer [[Nōshō Ōmura]], a Hōfu native. A commemorative festival is held annually on [[:en:January 23|January 23]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}. == Grounds == File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden (main sanctuary) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), keidaisha.jpg|Keidaisha (sub-shrine for the Inari deity) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|Torii (shrine gate) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=防府市史上巻|publisher=防府市教育委員会|date=1956-03-20|pages=196–197}} * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=続防府市史|publisher=続防府市史刊行会|date=1960-11-06|pages=244–246}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Saba-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-substub|pref=山口県}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Suō Province|Shrine 0]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|佐波神社 (防府市)|version=97878770|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Hōfu City]] [[Category:History of Hofu City]] {{wikidata link|Q38276448}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

In 1922, it was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine, and by 1924 it was designated to receive offerings from the state. A commemorative festival is held annually on [[:en:January 23|January 23]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}. == Grounds == File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden (main sanctuary) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), keidaisha.jpg|Keidaisha (sub-shrine for the Inari deity) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|Torii (shrine gate) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=防府市史上巻|publisher=防府市教育委員会|date=1956-03-20|pages=196–197}} * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=続防府市史|publisher=続防府市史刊行会|date=1960-11-06|pages=244–246}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Saba-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-substub|pref=山口県}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Suō Province|Shrine 0]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|佐波神社 (防府市)|version=97878770|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Hōfu City]] [[Category:History of Hofu City]] {{wikidata link|Q38276448}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Saba-jinja (Hofu)]][[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q38276448]] Wikidata name: Saba Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saba Shrine | image = File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg | location = 6-2 Sōja-chō, Hōfu, Yamaguchi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34.06125|N|131.582556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | deity = | shrine_Rank = [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]] [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]]
Former [[Kensha]] (Prefectural Shrine) | established= | architecture= | alternate_names = Sōja Kanekiri-sha | festivals = [[:en:April 2|April 2]] | rites = | prefecture= {{ill|Hofu|WD=Q502209}} }} '''Saba Shrine''' (佐波神社, ''Saba Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Sōja-chō, [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. It served as the [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] (provincial shrine) of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]] and was classified as a [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == Situated at the southern foot of Tatara Mountain, the shrine venerates a pantheon of fourteen deities, including [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Mihotsuhime|Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Shinatsuhiko|Shinatsuhiko]], Shinatsuhime, [[Ukano Mitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsuo, Nakatsutsuo, Uwatsutsuo), [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Takeminakata]], [[Takemikazuchi]], and [[Futsunushi-no-Kami]]. Originally known as ''Sōja Kanekiri-sha,'' the shrine was not listed in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' and is classified as an [[Shikigai Shrine|unranked shrine]]. According to legend, Emperor [[Chūai]] and Empress [[:en:Jingū|Jingū]] enshrined the gods here to pray for victory during their campaign against the [[Kumaso]]. The name ''Kanekiri'' ("metal cutting") is thought to symbolize the act of pacifying (cutting down) resistance during the [[Three Han Campaign|expedition to the Three Han]] regions, reflecting the [[Yin-Yang Five Phases|Five Elements theory]] that associates Korea with the "metal" direction御園生翁甫編『防府市史上巻』pp. 196–197. As a [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]], the shrine was established as a central place for provincial worship where the deities of all local shrines could be venerated. The exact date of its establishment is unclear but is presumed to have been after the founding of the [[:en:Suō Provincial Capital|provincial capital]]. In 1907, the shrine merged with the village shrines [[Katsuma Shrine]], [[:en:Hiyoshi Shrine|Hiyoshi Shrine]], and [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], adopting the name ''Saba Shrine.'' In 1922, it was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine, and by 1924 it was designated to receive offerings from the state. Renovations to the shrine buildings were carried out during this period御園生翁甫編『続防府市史』pp. 244–246. The shrine grounds feature a memorial and song monument dedicated to the composer [[Nōshō Ōmura]], a Hōfu native. A commemorative festival is held annually on [[:en:January 23|January 23]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}. == Grounds == File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden (main sanctuary) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), keidaisha.jpg|Keidaisha (sub-shrine for the Inari deity) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|Torii (shrine gate) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=防府市史上巻|publisher=防府市教育委員会|date=1956-03-20|pages=196–197}} * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=続防府市史|publisher=続防府市史刊行会|date=1960-11-06|pages=244–246}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Saba-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-substub|pref=山口県}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Suō Province|Shrine 0]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category: Completion tier 5]] }} [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] [[Category:Added translation template]]{{translated page|ja|佐波神社 (防府市)|version=97878770|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Architecture of Hōfu City]] [[Category:History of Hofu City]] {{wikidata link|Q38276448}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

It served as the [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] (provincial shrine) of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]] and was classified as a [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == Situated at the southern foot of Tatara Mountain, the shrine venerates a pantheon of fourteen deities, including [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Mihotsuhime|Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Shinatsuhiko|Shinatsuhiko]], Shinatsuhime, [[Ukano Mitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsuo, Nakatsutsuo, Uwatsutsuo), [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Takeminakata]], [[Takemikazuchi]], and [[Futsunushi-no-Kami]]. Originally known as ''Sōja Kanekiri-sha,'' the shrine was not listed in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' and is classified as an [[Shikigai Shrine|unranked shrine]]. According to legend, Emperor [[Chūai]] and Empress [[:en:Jingū|Jingū]] enshrined the gods here to pray for victory during their campaign against the [[Kumaso]]. The name ''Kanekiri'' ("metal cutting") is thought to symbolize the act of pacifying (cutting down) resistance during the [[Three Han Campaign|expedition to the Three Han]] regions, reflecting the [[Yin-Yang Five Phases|Five Elements theory]] that associates Korea with the "metal" direction御園生翁甫編『防府市史上巻』pp. 196–197. As a [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]], the shrine was established as a central place for provincial worship where the deities of all local shrines could be venerated. The exact date of its establishment is unclear but is presumed to have been after the founding of the [[:en:Suō Provincial Capital|provincial capital]]. In 1907, the shrine merged with the village shrines [[Katsuma Shrine]], [[:en:Hiyoshi Shrine|Hiyoshi Shrine]], and [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], adopting the name ''Saba Shrine.'' In 1922, it was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine, and by 1924 it was designated to receive offerings from the state. Renovations to the shrine buildings were carried out during this period御園生翁甫編『続防府市史』pp. 244–246. The shrine grounds feature a memorial and song monument dedicated to the composer [[Nōshō Ōmura]], a Hōfu native. A commemorative festival is held annually on [[:en:January 23|January 23]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}. == Grounds == File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden (main sanctuary) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), keidaisha.jpg|Keidaisha (sub-shrine for the Inari deity) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|Torii (shrine gate) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=防府市史上巻|publisher=防府市教育委員会|date=1956-03-20|pages=196–197}} * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=続防府市史|publisher=続防府市史刊行会|date=1960-11-06|pages=244–246}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Saba-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-substub|pref=山口県}} {{Sōja shrines}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] {{translated page|ja|佐波神社 (防府市)|version=97878770|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q38276448}} [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Added translation template]] [[Category:Architecture of Hōfu City]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hofu City]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Suō Province|Shrine 0]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
