Renovations to the shrine buildings were carried out during this period御園生翁甫編『続防府市史』pp. 244–246. The shrine grounds feature a memorial and song monument dedicated to the composer [[Nōshō Ōmura]], a Hōfu native. A commemorative festival is held annually on [[:en:January 23|January 23]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}. == Grounds == File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden (main sanctuary) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), keidaisha.jpg|Keidaisha (sub-shrine for the Inari deity) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|Torii (shrine gate) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=防府市史上巻|publisher=防府市教育委員会|date=1956-03-20|pages=196–197}} * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=続防府市史|publisher=続防府市史刊行会|date=1960-11-06|pages=244–246}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Saba-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-substub|pref=山口県}} {{Sōja shrines}} [[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] {{translated page|ja|佐波神社 (防府市)|version=97878770|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q38276448}} [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Added translation template]] [[Category:Architecture of Hōfu City]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hofu City]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Suō Province|Shrine 0]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Saba-jinja (Hofu)]][[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Saba-jinja (Hofu)]][[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Saba-jinja (Hofu)]][[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q38276448]] Wikidata name: Saba Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saba Shrine | image = File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg | location = 6-2 Sōja-chō, Hōfu, Yamaguchi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34.06125|N|131.582556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | deity = | shrine_Rank = [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]] [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]]
Former [[Kensha]] (Prefectural Shrine) | established= | architecture= | alternate_names = Sōja Kanekiri-sha | festivals = [[:en:April 2|April 2]] | rites = | prefecture= {{ill|Hofu|WD=Q502209}} }} '''Saba Shrine''' (佐波神社, ''Saba Jinja'') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Sōja-chō, [[:en:Hōfu|Hōfu]], [[:en:Yamaguchi Prefecture|Yamaguchi Prefecture]]. It served as the [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]] (provincial shrine) of [[:en:Suō Province|Suō Province]] and was classified as a [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == Situated at the southern foot of Tatara Mountain, the shrine venerates a pantheon of fourteen deities, including [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Mihotsuhime|Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto]], [[:en:Shinatsuhiko|Shinatsuhiko]], Shinatsuhime, [[Ukano Mitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsuo, Nakatsutsuo, Uwatsutsuo), [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Takeminakata]], [[Takemikazuchi]], and [[Futsunushi-no-Kami]]. Originally known as ''Sōja Kanekiri-sha,'' the shrine was not listed in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' and is classified as an [[Shikigai Shrine|unranked shrine]]. According to legend, Emperor [[Chūai]] and Empress [[:en:Jingū|Jingū]] enshrined the gods here to pray for victory during their campaign against the [[Kumaso]]. The name ''Kanekiri'' ("metal cutting") is thought to symbolize the act of pacifying (cutting down) resistance during the [[Three Han Campaign|expedition to the Three Han]] regions, reflecting the [[Yin-Yang Five Phases|Five Elements theory]] that associates Korea with the "metal" direction御園生翁甫編『防府市史上巻』pp. 196–197. As a [[:en:Sōja|Sōja]], the shrine was established as a central place for provincial worship where the deities of all local shrines could be venerated. The exact date of its establishment is unclear but is presumed to have been after the founding of the [[:en:Suō Provincial Capital|provincial capital]]. In 1907, the shrine merged with the village shrines [[Katsuma Shrine]], [[:en:Hiyoshi Shrine|Hiyoshi Shrine]], and [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], adopting the name ''Saba Shrine.'' In 1922, it was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine, and by 1924 it was designated to receive offerings from the state. Renovations to the shrine buildings were carried out during this period御園生翁甫編『続防府市史』pp. 244–246. The shrine grounds feature a memorial and song monument dedicated to the composer [[Nōshō Ōmura]], a Hōfu native. A commemorative festival is held annually on [[:en:January 23|January 23]]{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}. == Grounds == File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden (main sanctuary) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), keidaisha.jpg|Keidaisha (sub-shrine for the Inari deity) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|Torii (shrine gate) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=防府市史上巻|publisher=防府市教育委員会|date=1956-03-20|pages=196–197}} * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=続防府市史|publisher=続防府市史刊行会|date=1960-11-06|pages=244–246}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Saba-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-substub|pref=山口県}} {{Sōja shrines}} [[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] {{translated page|ja|佐波神社 (防府市)|version=97878770|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q38276448}} [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Added translation template]] [[Category:Architecture of Hōfu City]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hofu City]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Suō Province|Shrine 0]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

A commemorative festival is held annually on {{ill|January 23|en|January 23|WD=Q2276|lt=January 23}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}. == Grounds == File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden (main sanctuary) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), keidaisha.jpg|Keidaisha (sub-shrine for the Inari deity) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|Torii (shrine gate) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=防府市史上巻|publisher=防府市教育委員会|date=1956-03-20|pages=196–197}} * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=続防府市史|publisher=続防府市史刊行会|date=1960-11-06|pages=244–246}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Saba-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-substub|pref=山口県}} {{Sōja shrines}} [[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] {{translated page|ja|佐波神社 (防府市)|version=97878770|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q38276448}} [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Added translation template]] [[Category:Architecture of Hōfu City]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hofu City]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Suō Province|Shrine 0]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Saba-jinja (Hofu)]][[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Saba-jinja (Hofu)]][[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Saba-jinja (Hofu)]][[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q38276448]] Wikidata name: Saba Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:59, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saba Shrine | image = File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg | location = 6-2 Sōja-chō, Hōfu, Yamaguchi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34.06125|N|131.582556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | deity = | shrine_Rank = {{ill|Suō Province|en|Suō Province|WD=Q1053037|lt=Suō Province}} {{ill|Sōja|en|Sōja|WD=Q873536|lt=Sōja}}
Former [[Kensha]] (Prefectural Shrine) | established= | architecture= | alternate_names = Sōja Kanekiri-sha | festivals = {{ill|April 2|en|April 2|WD=Q2511|lt=April 2}} | rites = | prefecture= {{ill|Hofu|WD=Q502209}} }} '''Saba Shrine''' (佐波神社, ''Saba Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Sōja-chō, {{ill|Hōfu|en|Hōfu|WD=Q502209|lt=Hōfu}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|WD=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}. It served as the {{ill|Sōja|en|Sōja|WD=Q873536|lt=Sōja}} (provincial shrine) of {{ill|Suō Province|en|Suō Province|WD=Q1053037|lt=Suō Province}} and was classified as a [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == Situated at the southern foot of Tatara Mountain, the shrine venerates a pantheon of fourteen deities, including [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Mihotsuhime|Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|en|Shinatsuhiko|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko}}, Shinatsuhime, [[Ukano Mitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsuo, Nakatsutsuo, Uwatsutsuo), [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Takeminakata]], [[Takemikazuchi]], and [[Futsunushi-no-Kami]]. Originally known as ''Sōja Kanekiri-sha,'' the shrine was not listed in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' and is classified as an [[Shikigai Shrine|unranked shrine]]. According to legend, Emperor [[Chūai]] and Empress [[:en:Jingū|Jingū]] enshrined the gods here to pray for victory during their campaign against the [[Kumaso]]. The name ''Kanekiri'' ("metal cutting") is thought to symbolize the act of pacifying (cutting down) resistance during the [[Three Han Campaign|expedition to the Three Han]] regions, reflecting the [[Yin-Yang Five Phases|Five Elements theory]] that associates Korea with the "metal" direction御園生翁甫編『防府市史上巻』pp. 196–197. As a {{ill|Sōja|en|Sōja|WD=Q873536|lt=Sōja}}, the shrine was established as a central place for provincial worship where the deities of all local shrines could be venerated. The exact date of its establishment is unclear but is presumed to have been after the founding of the {{ill|Suō Provincial Capital|en|Suō Provincial Capital|WD=Q11416957|lt=provincial capital}}. In 1907, the shrine merged with the village shrines [[Katsuma Shrine]], [[:en:Hiyoshi Shrine|Hiyoshi Shrine]], and [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], adopting the name ''Saba Shrine.'' In 1922, it was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine, and by 1924 it was designated to receive offerings from the state. Renovations to the shrine buildings were carried out during this period御園生翁甫編『続防府市史』pp. 244–246. The shrine grounds feature a memorial and song monument dedicated to the composer [[Nōshō Ōmura]], a Hōfu native. A commemorative festival is held annually on {{ill|January 23|en|January 23|WD=Q2276|lt=January 23}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}. == Grounds == File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden (main sanctuary) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), keidaisha.jpg|Keidaisha (sub-shrine for the Inari deity) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|Torii (shrine gate) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=防府市史上巻|publisher=防府市教育委員会|date=1956-03-20|pages=196–197}} * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=続防府市史|publisher=続防府市史刊行会|date=1960-11-06|pages=244–246}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Saba-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-substub|pref=山口県}} {{Sōja shrines}} [[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] {{translated page|ja|佐波神社 (防府市)|version=97878770|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q38276448}} [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Added translation template]] [[Category:Architecture of Hōfu City]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hofu City]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Suō Province|Shrine 0]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

A commemorative festival is held annually on {{ill|January 23|en|January 23|WD=Q2276|lt=January 23|1=January 23}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}. == Grounds == File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden (main sanctuary) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), keidaisha.jpg|Keidaisha (sub-shrine for the Inari deity) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|Torii (shrine gate) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=防府市史上巻|publisher=防府市教育委員会|date=1956-03-20|pages=196–197}} * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=続防府市史|publisher=続防府市史刊行会|date=1960-11-06|pages=244–246}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Saba-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-substub|pref=山口県}} {{Sōja shrines}} [[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] {{translated page|ja|佐波神社 (防府市)|version=97878770|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q38276448}} [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Added translation template]] [[Category:Architecture of Hōfu City]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hofu City]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Suō Province|Shrine 0]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Saba-jinja (Hofu)]][[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Saba-jinja (Hofu)]][[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Saba-jinja (Hofu)]][[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saba Shrine | image = File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg | location = 6-2 Sōja-chō, Hōfu, Yamaguchi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34.06125|N|131.582556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | deity = | shrine_Rank = {{ill|Suō Province|en|Suō Province|WD=Q1053037|lt=Suō Province|1=Suō Province}} {{ill|Sōja|en|Sōja|WD=Q873536|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja}}
Former [[Kensha]] (Prefectural Shrine) | established= | architecture= | alternate_names = Sōja Kanekiri-sha | festivals = {{ill|April 2|en|April 2|WD=Q2511|lt=April 2|1=April 2}} | rites = | prefecture= {{ill|Hofu|WD=Q502209|1=Hōfu}} }} '''Saba Shrine''' (佐波神社, ''Saba Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Sōja-chō, {{ill|Hōfu|en|Hōfu|WD=Q502209|lt=Hōfu|1=Hōfu}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|WD=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}. It served as the {{ill|Sōja|en|Sōja|WD=Q873536|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja}} (provincial shrine) of {{ill|Suō Province|en|Suō Province|WD=Q1053037|lt=Suō Province|1=Suō Province}} and was classified as a [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == Situated at the southern foot of Tatara Mountain, the shrine venerates a pantheon of fourteen deities, including [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Mihotsuhime|Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|en|Shinatsuhiko|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko|1=Shinatsuhiko}}, Shinatsuhime, [[Ukano Mitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsuo, Nakatsutsuo, Uwatsutsuo), [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Takeminakata]], [[Takemikazuchi]], and [[Futsunushi-no-Kami]]. Originally known as ''Sōja Kanekiri-sha,'' the shrine was not listed in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' and is classified as an [[Shikigai Shrine|unranked shrine]]. According to legend, Emperor [[Chūai]] and Empress [[:en:Jingū|Jingū]] enshrined the gods here to pray for victory during their campaign against the [[Kumaso]]. The name ''Kanekiri'' ("metal cutting") is thought to symbolize the act of pacifying (cutting down) resistance during the [[Three Han Campaign|expedition to the Three Han]] regions, reflecting the [[Yin-Yang Five Phases|Five Elements theory]] that associates Korea with the "metal" direction御園生翁甫編『防府市史上巻』pp. 196–197. As a {{ill|Sōja|en|Sōja|WD=Q873536|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja}}, the shrine was established as a central place for provincial worship where the deities of all local shrines could be venerated. The exact date of its establishment is unclear but is presumed to have been after the founding of the {{ill|Suō Provincial Capital|en|Suō Provincial Capital|WD=Q11416957|lt=provincial capital|1=Suō Provincial Capital}}. In 1907, the shrine merged with the village shrines [[Katsuma Shrine]], [[:en:Hiyoshi Shrine|Hiyoshi Shrine]], and [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], adopting the name ''Saba Shrine.'' In 1922, it was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine, and by 1924 it was designated to receive offerings from the state. Renovations to the shrine buildings were carried out during this period御園生翁甫編『続防府市史』pp. 244–246. The shrine grounds feature a memorial and song monument dedicated to the composer [[Nōshō Ōmura]], a Hōfu native. A commemorative festival is held annually on {{ill|January 23|en|January 23|WD=Q2276|lt=January 23|1=January 23}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}. == Grounds == File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden (main sanctuary) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), keidaisha.jpg|Keidaisha (sub-shrine for the Inari deity) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|Torii (shrine gate) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=防府市史上巻|publisher=防府市教育委員会|date=1956-03-20|pages=196–197}} * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=続防府市史|publisher=続防府市史刊行会|date=1960-11-06|pages=244–246}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Saba-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-substub|pref=山口県}} {{Sōja shrines}} [[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] {{translated page|ja|佐波神社 (防府市)|version=97878770|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q38276448}} [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Added translation template]] [[Category:Architecture of Hōfu City]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hofu City]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Suō Province|Shrine 0]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

The shrine grounds feature a memorial and song monument dedicated to the composer [[Nōshō Ōmura]], a Hōfu native. A commemorative festival is held annually on {{ill|January 23|en|January 23|WD=Q2276|lt=January 23|1=January 23}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}. == Grounds == File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden (main sanctuary) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), keidaisha.jpg|Keidaisha (sub-shrine for the Inari deity) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|Torii (shrine gate) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=防府市史上巻|publisher=防府市教育委員会|date=1956-03-20|pages=196–197}} * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=続防府市史|publisher=続防府市史刊行会|date=1960-11-06|pages=244–246}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Saba-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-substub|pref=山口県}} {{Sōja shrines}} [[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] {{translated page|ja|佐波神社 (防府市)|version=97878770|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q38276448}} [[Category: Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Architecture of Hōfu City]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hofu City]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Suō Province|Shrine 0]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Saba-jinja (Hofu)]][[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Saba-jinja (Hofu)]][[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Saba-jinja (Hofu)]][[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Saba Shrine | image = File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg | location = 6-2 Sōja-chō, Hōfu, Yamaguchi Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34.06125|N|131.582556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi | deity = | shrine_Rank = {{ill|Suō Province|en|Suō Province|WD=Q1053037|lt=Suō Province|1=Suō Province}} {{ill|Sōja|en|Sōja|WD=Q873536|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja}}
Former [[Kensha]] (Prefectural Shrine) | established= | architecture= | alternate_names = Sōja Kanekiri-sha | festivals = {{ill|April 2|en|April 2|WD=Q2511|lt=April 2|1=April 2}} | rites = | prefecture= {{ill|Hofu|WD=Q502209|1=Hōfu}} }} '''Saba Shrine''' (佐波神社, ''Saba Jinja'') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Sōja-chō, {{ill|Hōfu|en|Hōfu|WD=Q502209|lt=Hōfu|1=Hōfu}}, {{ill|Yamaguchi Prefecture|en|Yamaguchi Prefecture|WD=Q127264|lt=Yamaguchi Prefecture|1=Yamaguchi Prefecture}}. It served as the {{ill|Sōja|en|Sōja|WD=Q873536|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja}} (provincial shrine) of {{ill|Suō Province|en|Suō Province|WD=Q1053037|lt=Suō Province|1=Suō Province}} and was classified as a [[Kensha]] (prefectural shrine) under the [[modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. == History == Situated at the southern foot of Tatara Mountain, the shrine venerates a pantheon of fourteen deities, including [[Amaterasu]], [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo]], [[Mihotsuhime|Mihotsuhime-no-Mikoto]], {{ill|Shinatsuhiko|en|Shinatsuhiko|WD=Q1073925|lt=Shinatsuhiko|1=Shinatsuhiko}}, Shinatsuhime, [[Ukano Mitama|Ukanomitama-no-Mikoto]], [[Sumiyoshi sanjin|Sumiyoshi Sanjin]] (Sokotsutsuo, Nakatsutsuo, Uwatsutsuo), [[Ōkuninushi]], [[Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|Kotoshironushi]], [[Takeminakata]], [[Takemikazuchi]], and [[Futsunushi-no-Kami]]. Originally known as ''Sōja Kanekiri-sha,'' the shrine was not listed in the ''[[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]'' and is classified as an [[Shikigai Shrine|unranked shrine]]. According to legend, Emperor [[Chūai]] and Empress [[:en:Jingū|Jingū]] enshrined the gods here to pray for victory during their campaign against the [[Kumaso]]. The name ''Kanekiri'' ("metal cutting") is thought to symbolize the act of pacifying (cutting down) resistance during the [[Three Han Campaign|expedition to the Three Han]] regions, reflecting the [[Yin-Yang Five Phases|Five Elements theory]] that associates Korea with the "metal" direction御園生翁甫編『防府市史上巻』pp. 196–197. As a {{ill|Sōja|en|Sōja|WD=Q873536|lt=Sōja|1=Sōja}}, the shrine was established as a central place for provincial worship where the deities of all local shrines could be venerated. The exact date of its establishment is unclear but is presumed to have been after the founding of the {{ill|Suō Provincial Capital|en|Suō Provincial Capital|WD=Q11416957|lt=provincial capital|1=Suō Provincial Capital}}. In 1907, the shrine merged with the village shrines [[Katsuma Shrine]], [[:en:Hiyoshi Shrine|Hiyoshi Shrine]], and [[Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]], adopting the name ''Saba Shrine.'' In 1922, it was elevated to the status of a prefectural shrine, and by 1924 it was designated to receive offerings from the state. Renovations to the shrine buildings were carried out during this period御園生翁甫編『続防府市史』pp. 244–246. The shrine grounds feature a memorial and song monument dedicated to the composer [[Nōshō Ōmura]], a Hōfu native. A commemorative festival is held annually on {{ill|January 23|en|January 23|WD=Q2276|lt=January 23|1=January 23}}{{Cite web|url=http://www.kanko-hofu.gr.jp/course/oomuranoushou|title=作曲家大村能章ゆかりのコース|publisher=防府市観光協会|accessdate=2017-07-08}}. == Grounds == File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden (main sanctuary) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), keidaisha.jpg|Keidaisha (sub-shrine for the Inari deity) File:Saba-jinja (Hofu), torii.jpg|Torii (shrine gate) == References == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=防府市史上巻|publisher=防府市教育委員会|date=1956-03-20|pages=196–197}} * {{Cite book|author=御園生翁甫|title=続防府市史|publisher=続防府市史刊行会|date=1960-11-06|pages=244–246}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Saba-jinja (Hofu)}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-substub|pref=山口県}} {{Sōja shrines}} [[ja:佐波神社 (防府市)]] {{translated page|ja|佐波神社 (防府市)|version=97878770|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q38276448}} [[Category:Architecture of Hōfu City]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Hofu City]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Suō Province|Shrine 0]] [[Category:Sōja shrines]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
